name: tiled-streaming

interface_signals.name: data_in
interface_signals.role: fixed-width channel tile beat (conv/relu/maxpool: input_width_bits == channel_tile*8 == 256; add: input_width_bits == 2*channel_tile*8 == 512, packed as {rhs_tile[255:0], lhs_tile[255:0]} with lhs in low bits)

interface_signals.name: data_out
interface_signals.role: fixed-width output channel tile beat (always channel_tile*8 == 256 bits regardless of op_type)

fit_constraints.max_on_chip_weight_bytes_note: Raised from the historical ZCU102-era 1 MiB cap to 4 MiB so the four biggest ResNet-50 spatial convs (node_conv_284/288/292/298, ~2.36 MiB each) fit under one uniform tiled-streaming contract on AMD Alveo U250 (75.9 Mbit BRAM + 22.4 MiB URAM). Smaller targets that need the original 1 MiB cap should clone this contract with a different name.
fit_constraints.bram_formula: line buffers store channel_tile lanes per spatial row; BRAM bits ~= channel_tile*8*image_width*(kernel_h-1) plus accumulator state.
buffer_sizing_rules: channel_tile defines channels per beat; CANONICAL value for ResNet-50 ABI is 32
for conv2d / relu / maxpool: input_width_bits == output_width_bits == channel_tile*8 == 256
for add: input_width_bits == 2*channel_tile*8 == 512 (concatenates two 256-bit tile halves: lhs in [255:0], rhs in [511:256]); output_width_bits == 256
spatial ops must retain line-buffer/window state across beats for the same pixel

abi_rules.tile_order: channel-major within a pixel: tile T contains channels [T*channel_tile .. (T+1)*channel_tile - 1]; tiles are emitted in ascending T order; pixels emitted in raster order (row-major: pixel_h outer, pixel_w inner)
abi_rules.byte_layout_in_beat: within one 256-bit data beat, channel c (relative to the tile, c in [0, channel_tile)) lives at bits [c*8 +: 8] as a signed INT8 (byte 0 at bits [7:0], byte 31 at bits [255:248]). This matches the existing 01_context.md byte-order convention.
abi_rules.add_lhs_rhs_alignment: in data_in for add: bits [255:0] = lhs_tile (matching the producer of mainSource), bits [511:256] = rhs_tile (matching the producer of skipSource). The two sides MUST present the same (pixel, tile_idx) in lock-step; valid_in fires only when both halves are present.
abi_rules.valid_ready_handshake: valid_in pulses once per accepted beat; ready_in MUST be honored (no in-flight beat dropped). valid_out is the same shape on the output side. No fire-and-forget pulses; if downstream not ready, hold valid_out.
abi_rules.conv_ic_tile_accumulation: for conv2d: across input-channel tiles for the SAME (pixel, kh, kw), the layer must accumulate partial sums in an internal accumulator. The accumulator clears when starting a new output pixel (or new oc_pass for tiled-output layers).
abi_rules.relu_maxpool_tile_cadence: for relu/maxpool: output one tile beat for each input tile beat in lock-step. Maxpool with stride > 1 still emits all tile beats of the kept pixels; it does NOT collapse channel dimensions.
abi_rules.no_dram_ports: tiled-streaming has NO DRAM AXI4 weight-read ports. Weights are loaded from on-chip BRAM via $readmemh of the LayerIR weights_path. Modules that need DRAM weights must use a different contract.
abi_rules.deterministic_emission: given the same input beat sequence, the output beat sequence MUST be deterministic and byte-exact across runs. No randomness, no time-of-day, no clock-domain skew.

protocol_rules: Foundry MUST set input_width_bits/output_width_bits from the LayerIR verbatim. For tiled-streaming these will be 256 (conv/relu/maxpool) or 512 (add input only) per the buffer_sizing_rules above.
Foundry MUST emit channel-major tile order matching abi_rules.tile_order. Any other order breaks chain coherence.
Foundry MUST NOT add DRAM AXI ports under tiled-streaming. If the layer's weight tensor exceeds max_on_chip_weight_bytes, the orchestrator escalates to a different contract.
Foundry MUST emit deterministic output for the same input sequence (golden-comparison gate enforces this).
